content:
# Geosteering

**Summary**: The process of adjusting the trajectory of a horizontal well to stay within the target reservoir zone.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2011-al-dhufairi|AL-DHUFAIRI, 2011]] evaluated geosteering effectiveness using mobility profile data.

- [[2011-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2011]] utilized geosteering with live monitoring to optimize the exploitation of remaining producible oil in the mother-Field.

## Scientific Context

- [[2011-al-dhufairi|AL-DHUFAIRI, 2011]] Crucial for maximizing recovery in horizontal wells with complex features like tar mats.

- [[2011-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2011]] utilized geosteering with live monitoring to optimize the exploitation of remaining producible oil in the mother-Field.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2011-al-dhufairi|AL-DHUFAIRI, 2011]] Guided by MWD tools and formation testing data.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Geosteering with formation tester tools is expensive and time-consuming, leading to the use of AI for prediction. ([[2011-al-dhufairi|AL-DHUFAIRI, 2011, p. 15]])


- Enabled the steering of wells into high-porosity zones and improved reservoir development planning. ([[2011-al-amri|Al-Amri, 2011, p. 32]])
